options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: starting_song_count
options.description: The number of songs that will be automatically unlocked at the start of a run.

options.name: additional_song_count
options.description:
The total number of songs that will be placed in the randomization pool.
- This does not count any starting songs or the goal song.
- The final song count may be lower due to other settings.

options.name: music_sheet_count_percentage
options.description:
Controls how many music sheets are added to the pool based on the number of songs, including starting songs.

Higher numbers leads to more consistent game lengths, but will cause individual music sheets to be less important.

options.name: music_sheet_win_count_percentage
options.description: The percentage of Music Sheets in the item pool that are needed to unlock the winning song.

options.name: allow_just_as_planned_dlc_songs
options.description: This Option has been Removed.

options.name: available_trap_types
options.description: This Option has been Removed.

options.name: dlc_packs
options.description:
Choose which DLC Packs will be included in the pool of chooseable songs.

Note: The [Just As Planned] DLC contains all [Muse Plus] songs.

options.name: streamer_mode_enabled
options.description:
In Muse Dash, an option named 'Streamer Mode' removes songs which may trigger copyright issues when streaming.

If this is enabled, only songs available under Streamer Mode will be available for randomization.

options.name: include_songs
options.description:
These songs will be guaranteed to show up within the seed.
- You must have the DLC enabled to play these songs.
- Difficulty options will not affect these songs.
- If there are too many included songs, this will act as a whitelist ignoring song difficulty.

options.name: exclude_songs
options.description:
These songs will be guaranteed to not show up within the seed.

Note: Does not affect songs within the "Include Songs" list.

options.name: goal_song
options.description:
One of the selected songs will be guaranteed to show up as the final Goal Song.
- You must have the DLC enabled to play these songs.
- If no songs are chosen, then the song will be randomly chosen from the available songs.

options.name: song_difficulty_mode
options.description:
Ensures that at any chosen song has at least 1 value falling within these values.
- Any: All songs are available
- Easy: 1, 2 or 3
- Medium: 4, 5
- Hard: 6, 7
- Expert: 8, 9
- Master: 10+
- Manual: Uses the provided minimum and maximum range.

options.name: song_difficulty_min
options.description:
Ensures that 1 difficulty has at least 1 this value or higher per song.

Note: Difficulty Mode must be set to Manual.

options.name: song_difficulty_max
options.description:
Ensures that 1 difficulty has at least 1 this value or lower per song.

Note: Difficulty Mode must be set to Manual.

options.name: grade_needed
options.description:
Completing a song will require a grade of this value or higher in order to unlock items.
The grades are as follows:
- Silver S (SS): >= 95% accuracy
- Pink S (S): >= 90% accuracy
- A: >= 80% or a Full Combo
- B: >= 70%
- C: >= 60%

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: chosen_traps
options.description:
This controls the types of traps that can be added to the pool.
- Traps last the length of a song, or until you die.
- VFX Traps consist of visual effects that play over the song. (i.e. Grayscale.)
- SFX Traps consist of changing your sfx setting to one possibly more annoying sfx.

Note: SFX traps are only available if [Just as Planned] DLC songs are enabled.

options.name: trap_count_percentage
options.description: This controls how many traps to add into the pool, based the total number of songs.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.
